title: Tirthan Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Tirthan Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: Tirthan Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in Himachal Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Tirthan Wildlife Sanctuary is a wildlife sanctuary in Himachal Pradesh, India. It is situated near the banks of the Tirthan river. It features densely forested areas and hosts a diverse fauna. In 2021, it was ranked the Best Managed Protected Area of India as per the Management Effectiveness Evaluation.

topPlaces.name: Renuka Ji
topPlaces.description: Sri Renukaji (also spelled Renuka Ji) is a Hindu shrine located on the shore of Renuka Lake in the Sirmaur district of Himachal Pradesh, India. The temple complex and adjacent wetland form a combined cultural, religious and ecological site centred on the veneration of the goddess Renuka, mother of the epic figure Parashurama.

items.note: Sri Renukaji (also spelled Renuka Ji) is a Hindu shrine located on the shore of Renuka Lake in the Sirmaur district of Himachal Pradesh, India. faq.q: What is the best time to visit Tirthan Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Tirthan Wildlife Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Tirthan Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kullu-Manali Airport (Bhuntar) (47 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (125 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Tirthan Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Tirthan Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Tirthan Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Tirthan Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Tirthan Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Renuka Ji.
